dsl_rule.translation_notes: Flip-flop logic implemented using Bollinger Bands (30-period SMA ± 1.5 std dev approximated by bb_upper/bb_lower). Entry: long when price crosses upper band upward; short when price crosses lower band downward. Exit: position reverses when opposite threshold is crossed (hard stop at opposite band). This creates mutually exclusive states with hysteresis. Context-dependent direction since signal switches between long and short based on threshold crossings.

backtest_rule.reason: Flip-flop logic implemented using Bollinger Bands (30-period SMA ± 1.5 std dev approximated by bb_upper/bb_lower). Entry: long when price crosses upper band upward; short when price crosses lower band downward. Exit: position reverses when opposite threshold is crossed (hard stop at opposite band). This creates mutually exclusive states with hysteresis. Context-dependent direction since signal switches between long and short based on threshold crossings.

source_rule_text: Use flip-flop logic with mutually exclusive threshold crossings to generate binary state transitions: maintain a directional signal until the opposite threshold is crossed in the opposite direction.
